exposed - `Dose` : Exposure dose (mg/kg) - `ID_recipient` : Recipient in which the soil was prepared - `ID_camp` : ID of the earthworm campagne and generation (see XXX for more information) - `ID` : ID of the earthworm - `Nb_rep` : Identification number of the replicate - `Time_point` : Time point to which the earthworm is dedicated - `Phase` : Uptake or Elimination phase - `Time_introduction_D0` : Time of the day when the earthworm was introduced into his contaminated cosm - `w0` : Body weight at t0 - `w_Tp` : Body weight at the given time point - `w_frozen` : Body weight before congelation - `C_worm` : Measured concentration of the substance in the earthworm - `C_soil` : Measured concentration of the substance in the soil at the given time point ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} Points represent the experimental data, the blue curve and the transparent blue band are produced with geom_smooth.](BB_TK_EPX_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-1-1.png){width=768} ::: ::: ## Modeling of the toxicokinetics Names of models : - C1 : One compartement only. - C1P : One compartment and exposure in Petri dishes via gut content. - C2 : Two compartements. - C2P : Two compartments and exposure in Petri dishes via gut content. ### In files creation for MCMC ::: {.cell code-show='show'} ```{.r .cell-code} Molecule <- "EPX" ########## C1 ################################################################ path_mod_EPX_C1 <- here::here("mod/TK_EPX_C1") path_fig_C1 <- here::here("fig/TK_single/EPX_C1/") Nb_Iter <- 100000 seeds <- c(C1 = 3333, C2 = 6656, C3 = 111222) text_priors <- "Distrib(kuEPX, LogUniform, 0.00001, 10000); # min, max Distrib(keEPX, LogUniform, 0.00001, 10000); # min, max Distrib(a_growth, TruncNormal, 0.005, 0.005, -0.5, 1); # mean, sd, min, max Distrib (Vr_a_growth, TruncNormal, 0.0015, 0.01, 0, 1); Distrib(Sigma_W, TruncNormal, 0.03, 0.01, 0.0001, 1); Distrib(Sigma_CiEPX, TruncNormal, 3.0, 3.0, 1, 1000);" text_likelihood <- "Likelihood(CiEPX, LogNormal, Prediction(CiEPX), Sigma_CiEPX); # Calcul des vraisemblances (geometric mean, geometric standard deviation) Likelihood(Weight, Normal, Prediction(Weight), Sigma_W); # Calcul des vraisemblances" text_param_ind <- "Distrib(a_growth, TruncNormal, a_growth, Vr_a_growth, -1.0, 1);" f_In_tot(path_mod_EPX_C1, Molecule, text_priors, text_likelihood, text_param_ind, Nb_Iter, seeds) ########## C1P ################################################################ path_mod_EPX_C1P <- here::here("mod/TK_EPX_C1P") path_fig_C1P <- here::here("fig/TK_single/EPX_C1P/") Nb_Iter <- 100000 seeds <- c(C1 = 333266, C2 = 660660, C3 = 110110) # Done on CEA servers (CCRT) text_priors <- "Distrib(kuEPX, LogUniform, 0.00001, 100000); # min, max Distrib(keEPX, LogUniform, 0.00001, 100000); # min, max Distrib(phi, Uniform, 0.0, 1); # min, max Distrib(a_growth, TruncNormal, 0.005, 0.005, -0.5, 1); # mean, sd, min, max Distrib (Vr_a_growth, TruncNormal, 0.0015, 0.01, 0, 1); Distrib(Sigma_W, TruncNormal, 0.03, 0.01, 0.0001, 1); Distrib(Sigma_CiEPX, TruncNormal, 3.0, 3.0, 1, 1000);" text_likelihood <- "Likelihood(CiEPX, LogNormal, Prediction(CiEPX), Sigma_CiEPX); # Calcul des vraisemblances (geometric mean, geometric standard deviation) Likelihood(Weight, Normal, Prediction(Weight), Sigma_W); # Calcul des vraisemblances" text_param_ind <- "Distrib(a_growth, TruncNormal, a_growth, Vr_a_growth, -1.0, 1);" f_In_tot(path_mod_EPX_C1P, Molecule, text_priors, text_likelihood, text_param_ind, Nb_Iter, seeds) ########## C2 ################################################################ Molecule <- "EPX" path_mod_EPX_C2 <- here::here("mod/TK_EPX_C2") path_fig_C2 <- here::here("fig/TK_single/EPX_C2/") Nb_Iter <- 100000 seeds <- c(C1 = 33033, C2 = 6666, C3 = 1201) text_priors <- "Distrib(kuEPX, LogUniform, 0.00001, 100000); # min, max Distrib(keEPX, LogUniform, 0.00001, 100000); # min, max Distrib(kperiph, LogUniform, 0.00001, 100000); # min, max Distrib(a_growth, TruncNormal, 0.005, 0.005, -0.5, 1); # mean, sd, min, max Distrib (Vr_a_growth, TruncNormal, 0.0015, 0.01, 0, 1); Distrib(Sigma_W, TruncNormal, 0.03, 0.01, 0.0001, 1); Distrib(Sigma_CiEPX, TruncNormal, 3.0, 3.0, 1, 1000);" text_likelihood <- "Likelihood(CiEPX, LogNormal, Prediction(CiEPX), Sigma_CiEPX); # Calcul des vraisemblances (geometric mean, geometric standard deviation) Likelihood(Weight, Normal, Prediction(Weight), Sigma_W); # Calcul des vraisemblances" text_param_ind <- "Distrib(a_growth, TruncNormal, a_growth, Vr_a_growth, -1.0, 1);" f_In_tot(path_mod_EPX_C2, Molecule, text_priors, text_likelihood, text_param_ind, Nb_Iter, seeds) ########## C2P ################################################################ path_mod_EPX_C2P <- here::here("mod/TK_EPX_C2P") path_fig_C2P <- here::here("fig/TK_single/EPX_C2P/") Nb_Iter <- 100000 seeds <- c(C1 = 333266, C2 = 660660, C3 = 110110) # Done on CEA servers (CCRT) text_priors <- "Distrib(kuEPX, LogUniform, 0.00001, 100000); # min, max Distrib(keEPX, LogUniform, 0.00001, 100000); # min, max Distrib(kperiph, LogUniform, 0.00001, 100000); # min, max Distrib(phi, Uniform, 0.0, 1); # min, max Distrib(a_growth, TruncNormal, 0.005, 0.005, -0.5, 1); # mean, sd, min, max Distrib (Vr_a_growth, TruncNormal, 0.0015, 0.01, 0, 1); Distrib(Sigma_W, TruncNormal, 0.03, 0.01, 0.0001, 1); Distrib(Sigma_CiEPX, TruncNormal, 3.0, 3.0, 1, 1000);" text_likelihood <- "Likelihood(CiEPX, LogNormal, Prediction(CiEPX), Sigma_CiEPX); # Calcul des vraisemblances (geometric mean, geometric standard deviation) Likelihood(Weight, Normal, Prediction(Weight), Sigma_W); # Calcul des vraisemblances" text_param_ind <- "Distrib(a_growth, TruncNormal, a_growth, Vr_a_growth, -1.0, 1);" f_In_tot(path_mod_EPX_C2P, Molecule, text_priors, text_likelihood, text_param_ind, Nb_Iter, seeds) # ./mcsim.m_TK_EPX TK_EPX_C1.in ``` ::: An example of how to run the models MCMC is given in the bash code block below. You need to be in the right folder to launch a given model. ::: {.cell execute='false'} ```{.bash .cell-code} pwd cd .. cd .. cd mod/TK_EPX pwd makemcsim m_TK_EPX.model & for j in $(seq 1 3); do ./mcsim.m_TK_EPX TK_EPX_C${j}.in done ``` ::: The first step is to retrieve the information of the MCMC simulation experiments. The inputs : - Input files must a name like "xxxxx.1.in" - Output files must a name like "xxxxx.1.out" ### Get MCMC results ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ########## C1 ################################################################ MCMC_out_EPX_C1 <- f_MCSim_ind(path_mod_EPX_C1) NC_C1 <- MCMC_out_EPX_C1$NC Nb_param_C1 <- MCMC_out_EPX_C1$Nb_param Nb_experiment_C1 <- MCMC_out_EPX_C1$Nb_experiment Nb_iter_kept_C1 <- MCMC_out_EPX_C1$Nb_iter_kept N_iter_setpoint_C1 <- MCMC_out_EPX_C1$N_iter_setpoint L_parameters_C1 <- unique(MCMC_out_EPX_C1$Priors$Nom) ########## C1P ################################################################ MCMC_out_EPX_C1P <- f_MCSim_ind(path_mod_EPX_C1P) NC_C1P <- MCMC_out_EPX_C1P$NC Nb_param_C1P <- MCMC_out_EPX_C1P$Nb_param Nb_experiment_C1P <- MCMC_out_EPX_C1P$Nb_experiment Nb_iter_kept_C1P <- MCMC_out_EPX_C1P$Nb_iter_kept N_iter_setpoint_C1P <- MCMC_out_EPX_C1P$N_iter_setpoint L_parameters_C1P <- unique(MCMC_out_EPX_C1P$Priors$Nom) ########## C2 ################################################################ MCMC_out_EPX_C2 <- f_MCSim_ind(path_mod_EPX_C2) NC_C2 <- MCMC_out_EPX_C2$NC Nb_param_C2 <- MCMC_out_EPX_C2$Nb_param Nb_experiment_C2 <- MCMC_out_EPX_C2$Nb_experiment Nb_iter_kept_C2 <- MCMC_out_EPX_C2$Nb_iter_kept N_iter_setpoint_C2 <- MCMC_out_EPX_C2$N_iter_setpoint L_parameters_C2 <- unique(MCMC_out_EPX_C2$Priors$Nom) ########## C2P ################################################################ MCMC_out_EPX_C2P <- f_MCSim_ind(path_mod_EPX_C2P) NC_C2P <- MCMC_out_EPX_C2P$NC Nb_param_C2P <- MCMC_out_EPX_C2P$Nb_param Nb_experiment_C2P <- MCMC_out_EPX_C2P$Nb_experiment Nb_iter_kept_C2P <- MCMC_out_EPX_C2P$Nb_iter_kept N_iter_setpoint_C2P <- MCMC_out_EPX_C2P$N_iter_setpoint L_parameters_C2P <- unique(MCMC_out_EPX_C2P$Priors$Nom) ``` ::: We can retrieve different information from the models : - C1 - The number of chains used : 3 - The number of parameters : 6 - The number of experiments : 71 - The number of last iterations used to describe the posterior distribution : 10^{4} - The number of last iterations used to create the `tab_setpoint.out` file : 333 - C1P - The number of parameters : 7 - C2 - The number of parameters : 7 - C2P - The number of parameters : 8 ### Priors used for the model ::: panel-tabset ## C1 ::: {.cell tbl-cap='Priors used for the model' tbl-label='tbl-EPXPriorsC1'} ```{.r .cell-code} colnames(MCMC_out_EPX_C1$Priors) <- c("Nom", "Distribution", "P1", "P2", "P3", "P4") MCMC_out_EPX_C1$Priors |> datatable( options = list( dom = 't', autoWidth = TRUE, columnDefs = list( list(className = 'dt-left', targets = "_all") ) ), class="hover" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} #MCMC_out_EPX$Priors ``` ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} n_samples <- 1e6 # Simulation of the distribution col_prior <- Nord_polar[4] alpha_prior <- 0.5 df_priors_dist_C1 <- MCMC_out_EPX_C1$Priors |> dplyr::select(Nom, Distribution, P1, P2, P3, P4) |> rowwise() |> mutate(samples = case_when( Distribution == "Uniform" ~ list(runif(n_samples, min = P1, max = P2)), Distribution == "Normal" ~ list(rnorm(n_samples, mean = P1, sd = P2)), Distribution == "LogUniform" ~ list(exp(runif(n_samples, min = log(P1), max = log(P2)))), Distribution == "TruncNormal" ~ list(rtruncnorm(n_samples, a = P3, b = P4, mean = P1, sd = P2)) )) |> unnest(samples) |> filter(!(Nom %in% c("kuEPX", "keEPX","kcomp_in", "kcomp_out") & samples > 0.01)) p_priors <- ggplot() + facet_wrap(~Nom, scales = "free")+ stat_halfeye( data = df_priors_dist_C1, aes( x = samples ), alpha = alpha_prior, fill = col_prior, color = col_prior, normalize = "xy" ) + labs( title = "Priors distributions", x = "Parameter value", y = "Density" ) + theme_minimal() p_priors ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![Distributions of the priors.](BB_TK_EPX_files/figure-html/fig-EPXpriorC1-1.png){#fig-EPXpriorC1 width=768} ::: ::: ## C1P ::: {.cell tbl-cap='Priors used for the model' tbl-label='tbl-EPXPriorsC1P'} ```{.r .cell-code} colnames(MCMC_out_EPX_C1P$Priors) <- c("Nom", "Distribution", "P1", "P2", "P3", "P4") MCMC_out_EPX_C1P$Priors |> datatable( options = list( dom = 't', autoWidth = TRUE, columnDefs = list( list(className = 'dt-left', targets = "_all") ) ), class="hover" ) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

You need to be in the right folder to launch a given model. ::: {.cell execute='false'} ```{.bash .cell-code} pwd cd .. cd .. cd mod/TK_EPX pwd for j in $(seq 1 71); do ./mcsim.m_TK_EPX TK_EPX_Setpoint_ind_${j}.in done ``` ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} Nb_experiment <- Nb_experiment_C1 ######### Observed data ###################################################### dObs_ind <- df_TK_EPX |> mutate( ID = as.numeric(ID), Group_tmp = case_when( ID < 65 ~ "Uptake", (ID > 64 & ID <= 128) ~ "Elimination", ID > 128 ~ "Uptake bis"), Group = fct_relevel(Group_tmp, c("Uptake", "Elimination", "Uptake bis")) ) colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "Weight"] <- "Weight_obs" colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "CiEPX"] <- "CiEPX_obs" colnames(dObs_ind)[colnames(dObs_ind) == "t"] <- "Times" ############ C1 ############################################################## Sim.Res.ind_C1 <- lapply(1:Nb_experiment, function(j) { read_tsv(file.path(path_mod_EPX_C1, paste0("Setpoints_ind_", j, ".out"))) %>% mutate(sim = j) }) %>% bind_rows() df_mod_ind_C1 <- NULL for (i in 1:Nb_experiment_C1){ ID_i <- unique(dObs_ind$ID)[i] Sim.Res.ind_i <- subset(Sim.Res.ind_C1, sim == i) |> dplyr::select(where(~ !all(is.na(.x)))) dObs_i <- subset(dObs_ind, ID == ID_i) Time_pred = dObs_i$Time # !!!
